core_mechanism: assignment of an algebraic invariant (a cohomology/homology group built from the solutions of an associated dynamical/differential equation) to a geometric object, invariant under the natural equivalence, so that differing invariants certify inequivalence and support algorithmic classification of objects up to that equivalence
brief_reason: The abstract centers on symplectic cohomology and on (algorithmic) classification issues, i.e. constructing a computable cohomological invariant to distinguish/classify objects up to equivalence; being lecture-note/survey text it only coarsely specifies the machinery, so this is the most specific defensible invariant-assignment mechanism the text supports.
